entries.title: Fatal Tesla 'Autopilot' crash in Katy, Texas feeds federal self-driving probe
entries.summary: A Tesla Model 3 the driver says was on Autopilot crashed through a home near Katy, Texas on June 20, killing a 76-year-old woman inside, a real-world AI-driver-assist fatality now tied to NHTSA's 3.2-million-vehicle recall investigation.

links.title: ABC News: Tesla allegedly in autopilot mode crashes into Texas house, woman killed
Electrek: Tesla driver says it was on Autopilot before fatal Texas home crash
TechTimes: Katy crash hits 3.2 million-vehicle recall probe

entries.title: DOJ intervenes to kill NAACP Clean Air Act suit over xAI's Memphis data centers
entries.summary: The Justice Department moved (filing June 15) to dismiss the NAACP's lawsuit alleging xAI runs unpermitted gas turbines polluting majority-Black Memphis neighborhoods to power Grok, framing the data-center buildout as a national-security priority—an unusual government intervention on behalf of a frontier developer.

links.title: CNBC: DOJ asks court to toss NAACP air pollution suit against xAI
Quartz: DOJ moves to dismiss NAACP Clean Air Act lawsuit against xAI
The Hill: Trump DOJ intervenes in xAI air pollution case

entries.title: OpenAI threat report: PRC-linked influence operations targeting US AI and data-center debates
entries.summary: OpenAI's June 2026 threat report says it disrupted China-linked ChatGPT account clusters that tried to stir up US opposition to AI data centers, a window into how generative AI is being used in (largely ineffective) covert influence campaigns.

links.title: OpenAI: PRC-linked influence operations are targeting AI debates in the US

entries.title: Transformer co-author Noam Shazeer leaves Google for OpenAI
entries.summary: Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer—co-author of the 2017 'Attention Is All You Need' paper—is moving to OpenAI in a deal reported around $2.7 billion, the latest marquee talent shift concentrating frontier-AI expertise among a few labs.

links.title: CNBC: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer leaves for OpenAI
Neowin: Noam Shazeer is leaving Google for OpenAI

entries.title: OpenAI discloses multistate attorney-general investigation
entries.summary: OpenAI revealed it faces a coordinated investigation by multiple state attorneys general over product safety and consumer-protection concerns, escalating government scrutiny beyond Florida's standalone suit and signaling broader enforcement exposure for the company.

links.title: Inc.: OpenAI faces multiple legal investigations—what state AGs are demanding

entries.title: EU consultation on high-risk AI classification guidelines nears (extended) deadline
entries.summary: The European Commission's targeted consultation on draft guidelines for classifying high-risk AI systems under the AI Act—originally closing June 23—is being extended after industry requests, shaping how providers will determine their obligations.

links.title: European Commission: targeted consultation on high-risk AI classification guidelines
Hunton: Commission releases draft high-risk AI guidelines

entries.title: Paper: 'Towards Auditing AI Systems in the Wild'
entries.summary: A new arXiv paper argues that benchmark- and sandbox-based evaluation gives only a limited view of deployed AI behavior and proposes methods for auditing systems in real-world conditions—directly relevant to the evaluation gap and audit agendas.

entries.title: Paper: statistical decision audits for public frontier-AI evaluation archives
entries.summary: A new arXiv preprint applies Bayesian inference and winner's-curse corrections to public archives of frontier-AI evaluations, addressing how to make trustworthy claims from accumulated capability/safety test results.

links.title: arXiv 2606.17005: Bayesian Inference and Decision Audits for Public Archives of Frontier AI Evaluations

entries.title: Paper: DoubtProbe, a black-box jailbreak defense
entries.summary: Researchers propose DoubtProbe, an inference-time, black-box defense that uses structural verification and semantic auditing to detect and block jailbreaks—contributing to the robustness/red-teaming literature.

links.title: arXiv 2606.16527: DoubtProbe: Black-Box Jailbreak Defense via Structural Verification and Semantic Auditing

entries.title: Paper: defensive misdirection against automated attacks on agentic AI
entries.summary: A new arXiv study analyzes 'defensive misdirection' as a countermeasure to model-guided, automated prompt-injection attacks on agentic AI systems, probing robustness of LLM agents that interpret instructions and invoke tools.

links.title: arXiv 2606.20470: Analyzing Defensive Misdirection Against Model-Guided Automated Attacks on Agentic AI Systems
